question: Does self-improvement compound over time, or is it decelerating?
methodology: Measured DOMEX yield, throughput, L3+ rate, and principle extraction across 3 eras (S341-S400, S401-S450, S451-S468). Sources: SWARM-LANES.md, SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md, PRINCIPLES.md, SESSION-LOG.md, lesson files.
expect: All 3 quantity metrics decelerate (yield, throughput, principle rate). Compounding claim FALSIFIED.
actual: Quantity metrics decelerate monotonically (yield 1.03→0.82→0.59, lessons/session 5.9→4.8→3.6, merges/session 3.8→3.4→2.7). But quality metrics IMPROVE: K→P ratio 15.5→9.3→8.4 (each principle needs fewer lessons = more efficient extraction), L3+ rate among tagged lessons 21.5%→81.0% (confounded by infrastructure). Merge rate stable (93.5%→95.6%→94.1%).
diff: Prediction PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. Expected pure deceleration; found maturation pattern (quantity declines, quality improves). The swarm doesn't compound in volume — it compounds in density. This is r/K transition (L-1121), not decay.

findings.key_finding: Self-improvement shows maturation, not compounding or decay. Quantity decelerates (r→K transition, L-1121) while quality-per-unit improves. The swarm's knowledge density increases even as production volume declines. This is consistent with NK crystallization (L-1158) and attention carrying capacity (2.1x past threshold).
findings.falsification_result: PARTIAL — compounding in volume is FALSE (monotonic deceleration). Compounding in density is TRUE (K→P improving). Net: self-improvement is real but follows maturation curve, not exponential.
confounds: L3+ tagging infrastructure introduced mid-Era 2 (comparison unfair)
Concurrent sessions in Era 3 may deflate per-session metrics
Principle dedup changes accounting across eras

successor: Test prediction: Era 4 (S469-S518) yield should stabilize (not further decline) if maturation model is correct. If yield continues declining below 0.3 L/lane, maturation model is wrong and it's genuine decay.
